rulings.Case Title: DALE DUNCAN ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Motion Calendar for Friday, June 2, 2023, line 1. Defendant's Motion to be Relieved as Counsel is GRANTED. No opposition filed. Counsel to prepare order. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO VS. RICHARD KIWATA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Confirmation Of Referees Report Re Apportionment Of Fees And Expenses And Distribution Of Proceeds Of The Sale Of Real Property Discharge Of Referee
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Motion Calendar for Friday, June 2, 2023, line 2. Referee's Petition to Confirm Distribution of Sale Proceeds is CONTINUED to July 12, 2023, until after Howard Kiwata's scheduled request for court approval of attorney's fees and costs on June 28, 2023, is heard. (Supplemental Report, 3:4-5.) The Court request supplemental briefing, to be filed by June 30, 2023, but after the aforementioned June 28, 2023 hearing, to answer the following questions: (1) Referee maintains that $1,554,060.18 remains in total funds, but the proffered calculations only distribute $1,463,256.46. Please explain this discrepancy. (2) In Judge Tong's May 11, 2023 Judgment, $1,010.351.62 was awarded to the Hironaka Trust. Here, Referee recommends a distribution of $1,196,617.71 to the Hiranoka Trust. Where does the $186,266.09 excess distribution come from? (The Court is under the impression that $90,803.01 for attorney's fees makes up some of this difference, but what about the rest?) (3) How do the results of the June 28, 2023, hearing change or affect the distribution? Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: 57 TAYLOR I7, LP VS. FRANCISCO JUARES HERNANDEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Motion Calendar for Friday, June 2, 2023, line 7. Defendant's Motion to Set Aside Default is DENIED. Moving party failed to carry its burden under CCP 473(b). Motion is not supported by competent evidence. CCP 2015.5.
